hero.subtitle: Organize, review, and learn at your pace with the SM-P model. A smart system that adapts to your memory for extraordinary results.

features.smpSubtitle: Our intelligent algorithm predicts when you're about to forget a concept and reminds you at the exact right time.

adaptive.title: Adaptive Algorithm
adaptive.desc: Analyzes your response speed and accuracy to adjust review intervals automatically.

spaced.title: Spaced Repetition
spaced.desc: Optimizes long-term memory through strategic session scheduling.

progress.title: Real Progress
progress.desc: Visualize how your retention improves with detailed charts and personalized goals.

features.modesSubtitle: Not every day is the same. Choose the mode that best fits your energy and time.

normal.title: General Mode
normal.desc: The fastest way to stay up to date. Combines all pending cards from all decks in one optimized session.

hard.title: Intensive Mode
hard.desc: Focus on the difficult stuff. Select specific decks and practice only the cards you struggle with most.

casual.title: Casual Mode
casual.desc: Stress-free study. Review any content without affecting statistics or the SM-P algorithm intervals.

custom.title: Individual Deck
custom.desc: Total control. Study a single deck respecting your daily limits for new cards and reviews.

decks.title: Everything in your Decks
decks.subtitle: Create, import, and study with decks that adapt to you.

community.title: Free Decks
community.desc: Access thousands of decks created by experts and the community. From languages to medicine, everything ready to study.

media.title: Audio & Images
media.desc: Enrich your cards with images and audio. Upload your own files for deeper learning.

gamification.title: Gamification
gamification.desc: Earn points and level up with every card reviewed. Turn studying into a fun habit.

backup.title: Deck Backup
backup.desc: Your decks are always safe. Export and restore all your content whenever you want.
backup.note: The backup includes images and audio files attached to your cards.

stats.title: Know your real progress
stats.subtitle: You don't just study, you measure your evolution. Detailed statistics so you know exactly how you're advancing.

upcoming.title: Upcoming cards
upcoming.subtitle: Cards scheduled for the next 7 days

hard.title: Hard words
hard.note: Cards with lower retention are reviewed more frequently.

cta.title: Deep tracking of your learning
cta.desc: Get daily, weekly, and monthly reports of your real performance. Know how many cards await you tomorrow, which concepts need reinforcing, and how your retention evolves over time.
bullets: Retention rate by category and card

cta2.subtitle: Join thousands of students already optimizing their time and improving their results with RecallNest.

footer.tagline: Intelligent learning for a brighter future.
footer.made: Made with dedication for students around the world.

legal.title: Legal
legal.cookiesText: We use cookies mainly to remember your language preference and to understand how we can improve our app. Your privacy is important to us: we do not sell your data to anyone, nor do we use cookies for advertising. Cookies help us make your experience smoother, more personal, and more secure. By continuing to use RecallNest, you agree to our use of cookies for these essential purposes.

methodology.title: The 4 Pillars of RecallNest
methodology.subtitle: We merge neuroscience with technology to ensure that what you learn today, you remember forever.

p1.title: Comprehensible Input
p1.short: The key theory for acquiring languages naturally, without the pain of forced memorization.

p2.title: The Forgetting Curve
p2.short: We overcome the natural loss of information through strategic time interventions.

p3.title: Active Recall
p3.short: Don't read passively. Force your brain to retrieve information to strengthen connections.

p4.title: SRS (Spaced Repetition)
p4.short: An intelligent algorithm that personalizes your study intervals for total efficiency.

srs.title: SRS: The Time Algorithm
srs.subtitle: Understand how time and repetition transform your short-term memory into permanent knowledge.

algorithm.title: The Algorithm
algorithm.desc: The golden rule for efficient retention: increasing intervals or complete reset.

howItWorks.content: If you answer correctly, the interval increases (4 days, then 10 days, etc.). If you fail, the counter resets to ensure you relearn the concept from scratch.

lifeMoments.title: Life Moments
lifeMoments.desc: Integrate learning into your daily routine, from music practice to your commute to work.
lifeMoments.expanded: SRS is not just for vocabulary. Use it to remember chord progressions, mathematical formulas, or any information you need to retrieve in seconds.

longTerm.title: Your Long-Term Brain
longTerm.desc: Move information from temporary storage to your permanent "hard drive."

metaphor.content: Your brain prioritizes what you use frequently. SRS tricks your brain into believing that information is vital for survival, storing it in deep memory.

activeRecall.title: What is Active Recall?
activeRecall.subtitle: The most powerful study technique according to neuroscience for transferring information into your long-term memory.

vsCard.title: Passive vs. Active Study
vsCard.desc: Re-reading your notes creates an illusion of competence. Active Recall forces your brain to build the pathway to the information, strengthening it.

step1.title: Question
step1.desc: You see a concept or question without the answer in sight.

step2.title: Effort
step2.desc: Your brain intensely searches for the information within your neural connections.

step3.title: Connection
step3.desc: When you find it (or fail and then see the solution), the connection becomes 10x stronger.

neural.title: Neural Pathways
neural.desc: It’s like creating a path in a forest; the more you walk it, the easier it becomes to travel.

time.title: Efficiency
time.desc: You study for less time because every minute of effort is worth three times more than normal reading.

forgettingCurve.title: Understanding Forgetting
forgettingCurve.subtitle: Why do we forget so fast? Discover the science behind memory and how to combat it.

whatIs.title: What is it?
whatIs.desc1: It's the graph that shows how quickly we lose information if we don't try to remember it.
whatIs.desc2: Reality: we forget 50-80% of what we've learned in just 24 hours without review.
whatIs.desc3: The decline: the loss is exponential at first and then stabilizes.

names.title: Names
names.desc: You meet someone at a party and forget their name 5 minutes later.

conferences.title: Conferences
conferences.desc: You leave a keynote speech motivated, but the next day you don't remember the key points.

reading.title: Reading
reading.desc: You read an interesting article in a week and only remember a vague idea.

formula.title: The i + 1 Formula
formula.desc: It involves consuming content where you understand 80% through context and the remaining 20% is the new challenge (+1). You don't 'study' rules, you 'acquire' the language naturally.

child.title: How children learn
child.desc: You understood "give me the ball" because you saw the ball, not because you knew it was a direct object.

series.title: Familiar series
series.desc: Watching your favorite movie in another language. Since you already know the plot, your brain deduces new words.

reading.title: Graded reading
reading.desc: Books written specifically for your current level.

stress.title: Less stress
stress.desc: You replace grammar books with content you enjoy (YouTube, podcasts, comics).

patterns.title: Natural patterns
patterns.desc: You learn speech patterns directly from natives, without studying rigid grammatical rules.

output.title: The mystery of output
output.desc1: Output is the result, not the cause. Input is charging the battery (putting information in) and Output is using the energy (releasing what you've learned).
output.desc2: Key: if you speak too soon without enough input, you'll end up translating mentally instead of thinking in the language.
